name: iot_pwn
description: IoT/Router attack module: default credential attacks (SSH/Telnet/FTP/HTTP), SNMP community brute-force, UPnP exposure check, RTSP no-auth detection, HTTP admin panel discovery, router CVE checks
author: 0xb0rn3 | github.com/0xb0rn3

optional_dependencies.requests: HTTP admin panel discovery + HTTP Basic/Digest credential attacks - pip3 install requests
optional_dependencies.paramiko: SSH default credential attacks - pip3 install paramiko

help.description: IoT and router exploitation module. Tests default credentials across SSH, Telnet, FTP, and HTTP admin panels. Performs SNMP community string brute-force, UPnP SSDP exposure detection, RTSP no-auth checks, and known router CVE probing. Inspired by routersploit toolset features.

target.description: Target IP address of the IoT device or router

ssh.description: Test SSH default credentials using paramiko

http.description: Discover HTTP admin panels and test HTTP Basic/Digest default creds

snmp.description: Brute-force SNMP community strings over UDP/161

rtsp.description: Check if RTSP stream is accessible without authentication

upnp.description: Check for exposed UPnP SSDP service

max_creds.description: Maximum credential pairs to test (all by default)

notes: Default credentials list embedded from routersploit + additional IoT vendor defaults
SSH credential testing requires paramiko: pip3 install paramiko
HTTP checks require requests: pip3 install requests
SNMP brute-force uses raw UDP without external tools
UPnP check uses UDP SSDP M-SEARCH — no nmap required
All attacks are concurrent via ThreadPoolExecutor
Stop-on-success per service — stops trying creds once valid pair found
